mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, systematically compressing equity valuations and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance when unemployment remains above the threshold for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansions in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand recovery, boosting corporate earnings forecasts and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in over 55 percent of cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and higher capacity utilization, which signals near-term economic strength and drives positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive industrial production revisions are not significantly higher than baseline, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 195 trigger market expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65 percent of months where CPI exceeds the threshold, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for concurrent rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate indicates robust labor market tightening, supporting wage growth and consumer demand, which lifts equity valuations.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in over 50 percent of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, rising real GDP growth signals successful economic stimulus and expanding corporate profit margins, translating directly into positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive GDP revisions during easing periods do not yield statistically significant positive 60-day forward returns, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant industrial production alongside elevated inflation signals supply-side constraints and corporate margin compression, leading to risk-off sentiment and negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances, or the joint condition fails to produce a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.
